reforms.description: The new Fisheries Development Fund replaces the Fisheries Rationalisation Fund, providing grants to decommission a portion of Iceland's fishing fleet, thereby aiming to stabilize the fisheries sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing overcapacity in the fishing fleet, the fund aims to promote sustainable fishing practices, which can enhance long-term economic viability in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has begun providing grants to decommission parts of the fishing fleet.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at addressing overfishing and promoting sustainability in the fisheries sector, which is crucial for Iceland's economy.
reforms.source_quote: The new Fisheries Development Fund, which replaces the Fisheries Rationalisation Fund, has proffered grants of nearly IKr 3 billion to decommission some 8 per cent of Iceland's fishing fleet.
